title: Mentee Robotics — Acquisition $900M
excerpt: Announced at CES 2026 on January 6. Mobileye — Intel's autonomous driving subsidiary and the world's leading ADAS chip maker — acquires Mentee Robotics for $900M ($612M cash + ~26.2M Mobileye shares). Mentee was co-founded by Prof. Amnon Shashua (Mobileye's own founder and Intel EVP) and trains humanoids via human-to-robot mentoring: a single human demo is converted into millions of synthetic training trajectories, bypassing the need for large-scale real-world data collection. Roadmap: PoC deployments in fulfillment and assembly in 2026, first production batch in 2027, commercial scale in 2028, home use ~2030. The deal is a vertical integration play: Mobileye now controls both the perception silicon (EyeQ chips) and the humanoid AI stack, positioning it as a full-platform competitor to Nvidia's Isaac/GR00T ecosystem.

All major Series A/B investors including Sequoia, Coatue, Felicis, Samsung, LG, Salesforce Ventures, and CommonSpirit Health doubled down.
